title: Arosma acutifolia
taxonomy: Arosma acutifolia is classified under the family Anacardiaceae, commonly known as the cashew family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to tropical Africa, and Arosma acutifolia is one of the species within this genus. The classification of Arosma acutifolia can also be broken down into its botanical name, which is further divided into the species, genus, family, order, class, division, and kingdom levels. Specifically, Arosma acutifolia is a species in the genus Arosma, family Anacardiaceae, order Sapindales, class Magnoliopsida, division Magnoliophyta, and kingdom Plantae. This classification helps to understand the relationships between different plant species and their evolutionary history.

The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
